# crowci_global_registry (Resource)

Manages a global container registry credential on the Crow CI server.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "crowci_global_registry" "example" {
  address  = "docker.io"
  username = "myuser"
  password = "mypassword"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `address` (String) Registry address (e.g. 'docker.io', 'ghcr.io').
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Registry password. Not returned by the API after creation.
- `username` (String) Registry username.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Creation time as a Unix timestamp.
- `id` (Number) Registry ID assigned by Crow CI.
- `readonly` (Boolean) Whether the registry is read-only.
- `updated_at` (Number) Last update time as a Unix timestamp.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = crowci_global_registry.example
  id = "docker.io"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import crowci_global_registry.example docker.io
```
